settings.text: What a project looks like in practice.
Honest scope, clear process, and delivery you can hold. No tiered packages.

settings.title: Editorial
settings.description: Features and profiles for magazines, books, and independent publications. Shot in natural light, over unhurried mornings, with a small footprint on set.

settings.title: Portrait
settings.description: Individual and small-group portraiture for founders, creatives, and private clients. Studio or on location. One strong frame is worth more than fifty adequate ones.

settings.title: Lifestyle
settings.description: Families, couples, and documentary commissions. Unposed, patient, and built around the real rhythm of the day rather than a shot list.

settings.title: Commercial
settings.description: Small brand campaigns, still-life, and look books for independent makers. Editorial in spirit, practical in delivery, licensed cleanly.

settings.title: A small number of offerings, honestly scoped
Half-day session
settings.description: Four hours on location or in the studio. One or two setups, a measured pace, and a curated set of final images delivered as high-resolution files.

settings.title: Full-day session
settings.description: A full working day, suited to editorial features, multi-look portraiture, and small brand campaigns. Includes location scout, shoot, and delivery.

settings.title: Multi-day project
settings.description: Two or more shooting days for longer editorial work, interiors books, and family albums. Scoped per project — we talk it through before anything is booked.

settings.title: Print and album
settings.description: Prints up to A2 on archival cotton rag and hand-bound linen albums. Priced per item; most sessions include a small house-album by default.

settings.title: Inquiry
settings.description: You send a few lines about what you have in mind — who the shoot is for, a rough timeline, where it might happen. I reply within two working days.

settings.title: Brief
settings.description: A short call or exchange of notes. We talk through references, logistics, and any hard constraints. You get a written brief and a proposed date.

settings.title: Shoot
settings.description: The session itself. Unhurried, quiet, with minimal crew. Medium format, film or digital depending on the brief, always natural or window light by default.

settings.title: Edit
settings.description: I spend more time editing than shooting. You receive a curated selection within three weeks, with an option to request minor revisions to the final set.

settings.title: Deliver
settings.description: Final files via a private gallery, plus prints or a linen-bound album if that's part of the project. Licenses are simple, written, and specific to your use.

settings.question: What are your rates?
settings.answer: Session rates begin at a half-day rate and scale with scope. Every project is quoted in a written brief — there are no listed packages because every shoot is a little different.

settings.question: What's the turnaround?
settings.answer: Three weeks for a final curated set. Rush delivery is possible on editorial assignments; say so in the inquiry.

settings.question: Do you travel?
settings.answer: Yes — for editorial and commissioned work. Travel and accommodation are billed at cost and agreed in writing before the shoot.

settings.question: How do licensing and usage rights work?
settings.answer: Every commercial project ships with a written license that names the usage, territory, and term. Private-client work includes personal-use rights by default.

settings.title: Ask anything
settings.text:
If a question isn't covered here, send a line — I'd rather answer it directly than guess what it was.

hello@example.com
Send a few lines about timeline, location, and scope. I reply to everything.
